info.title: Citability API
info.description: Citability is a domain intelligence platform that measures AI visibility, citation readiness, and agentic commerce readiness across 607M domains. The API provides scanning, scoring, and enrichment endpoints for ADP (AI Discovery Protocol) compliance, llms.txt validation, Schema.org markup, MCP endpoint detection, and content extractability analysis.

get.summary: Get opportunity score for a domain
get.description:
Calculate opportunity score for a single domain.

    The score is based on:
    - Content signals (40%): Page count, English content, content size
    - Inverse authority (35%): Lower authority = higher opportunity
    - Growth trajectory (15%): Authority trend and content growth
    - Vertical affinity (10%): Target market fit

    Returns score 0-100 and tier classification.

post.description:
Calculate opportunity scores for multiple domains in batch.

    Maximum 100 domains per request.
    Uses default metric values for each domain.

schema.title: Response Score Domains Batch Api V1 Domain Batch Post

get.description:
Retrieve leads from enriched parquet data files.

    Supports filtering by:
    - vertical: Industry vertical (e.g., "SaaS", "E-commerce")
    - min_score: Minimum opportunity score (0-100)
    - max_score: Maximum opportunity score (0-100)
    - tier: Opportunity tier (tier_1, tier_2, tier_3)

    Supports pagination via limit and offset.
    Results are sorted by opportunity_score descending.

    Pass ?enrich=true to include WHOIS/DNS/tech stack data (slower).

parameters.name: enrich

schema.description: Include WHOIS/DNS/tech stack enrichment (slower, 5-15s per domain)
schema.title: Enrich

parameters.description: Include WHOIS/DNS/tech stack enrichment (slower, 5-15s per domain)

get.description: Get aggregate statistics about available leads.
Get list of unique verticals in the lead data.

schema.title: Response Get Verticals Api V1 Leads Verticals Get

get.description: Stream leads as a CSV file. Use ?enrich=true to include WHOIS/DNS data (slower). Max 1000 rows unenriched, 10000 enriched.

get.summary: Get full enrichment for a single domain
get.description: Returns WHOIS, DNS, email provider, hosting provider, CDN, SPF/DMARC flags. Slow (5-15s).

schema.title: Response Get Lead Enrichment Api V1 Leads  Domain  Enrichment Get

post.summary: Generate SEO tasks for a domain and add them to the kanban board
post.description:
Run a scan against the domain (or use cached results), generate SEO improvement
tasks from the recommendations, and persist them to the user's kanban board.

If project_id is omitted the user's first project is used. If no projects
exist, a 422 is returned asking the caller to create a project first.

get.summary: Scan single domain for ADP compliance
get.description:
Scan a domain for ADP (AI Discovery Protocol) compliance.

    Checks 20 ADP v2.1 endpoints across categories:
    - Core: ai.json, security.txt, robots.txt, ai-sitemap.xml, opensearch.xml
    - Feeds: feed.json, updates.json, rss.xml
    - AI Content: llms.txt, llms-full.txt, llms-lite.txt, ai-discovery.json/md, knowledge-graph.json
    - News: news/llms.txt, speakable.json, changelog.json, archive.jsonl
    - API: webhooks/discovery, adp/stats

    Returns compliance score (0-100) and level classification.

post.summary: Scan multiple domains for ADP compliance
post.description:
Batch scan multiple domains for ADP compliance.

    Maximum 50 domains per request.
    Scans are performed concurrently for efficiency.

schema.title: Response Scan Domains Batch Api V1 Scan Batch Post

post.description:
Perform a comprehensive unified scan combining:

    1. **Discovery Score** (0-100): ADP compliance + citation readiness
    2. **Agentic Score** (0-100): MCP + commerce protocol readiness
    3. **Citation Score** (0-100): Content quality for AI citation

    Runs all four scanners concurrently:
    - ADP Scanner (20 endpoints)
    - Emerging Standards Scanner (MCP, agents.json)
    - Commerce Readiness Scanner (UCP, A2A, ACP protocols)
    - Citation Readiness Scanner (content structure, info density, schema)

    Returns:
    - Three aggregate scores for the 3-pillar display
    - Detailed breakdowns from each scanner
    - Prioritized recommendations
    - CTA routing to Pressonify.ai or PresSEO based on gaps

get.summary: Get combined priority score for a domain
get.description:
Calculate combined priority score by internally calling both
    opportunity scoring and ADP compliance scanning.

    The priority score favors domains with:
    - High opportunity score (good content, growth trajectory)
    - Low ADP score (room for improvement)

    Also detects buying signals to help qualify leads:
    - high_opportunity_low_adp: Needs help, has potential
    - growth_ready_no_infrastructure: Growing but no ADP setup
    - blocking_ai_crawlers: Has robots.txt but blocks AI
    - partial_adp_implementation: Aware but incomplete
    - untapped_potential: Very low ADP with decent content
    - high_value_target: High opportunity overall
    - quick_win_optimization: Some infrastructure, needs tuning
Get list of all available industry vertical classifications.

schema.title: Response Get Verticals Api V1 Classify Verticals Get

get.description: Get statistics about the classification cache.

post.description: Clear the classification cache. Use with caution.

schema.title: Response Clear Cache Api V1 Classify Cache Clear Post

get.description:
Classify a domain into an industry vertical using AI.

    Available verticals:
    - SaaS, E-commerce, Agency, Media/Publishing, Developer Tools
    - Marketing, Productivity, Finance, Healthcare, Education, Other

    Uses LLM-powered classification with TLD-based fallback if AI is unavailable.
    Results are cached for performance (10K entry LRU cache).

post.description:
Classify multiple domains into industry verticals in batch.

    Maximum 100 domains per request.
    Uses parallel processing for efficiency.

get.summary: Browse all 607M Common Crawl domains with filters
get.description: Paginated browse over the full Common Crawl host index. Filter by authority (hcrank10), page count (fetch_200), TLD. Sort by any metric. Server-rendered and SEO-friendly. This is the REAL 607M domain directory — the /directory/browse endpoint above is the curated llms.txt subset.

get.summary: Export filtered CC browse results as CSV
get.description: Same filters as /cc-browse but returns a CSV stream. Free tier capped at 100 rows for anonymous users; authenticated users get up to 10,000.
Get detailed ADP information for a specific domain

post.description:
Generate a comprehensive report for a domain by aggregating data from multiple sources:

    **Data Sources:**
    - **Unified Scan**: ADP compliance, MCP readiness, commerce protocols, citation readiness
    - **Common Crawl**: Domain authority metrics (hcrank10, prank10)
    - **Domain Intel**: WHOIS registration, DNS records, email/hosting providers
    - **Free Enrichment**: Homepage scrape, Wayback Machine, certificate transparency
    - **Priority Scoring**: Lead qualification with detailed breakdown

    **Lead Tracking:**
    The email/domain pair is stored in the leads database for follow-up.

    **Response Time:**
    Typically 15-30 seconds. Timeouts applied to slow operations.

get.description: Returns an example full report structure for documentation purposes.

post.description: Sync user from Clerk webhook or first login.

List tasks, optionally filtered by domain.

post.description: Save a scan to user's dashboard.

post.description: Create improvement tasks from scan recommendations.

get.description:
Retrieve all projects for the authenticated user.

    Supports pagination via limit and offset parameters.
    Results are sorted by most recently updated first.

post.description:
Create a new project to monitor a domain's ADP compliance.

    The endpoint will:
    1. Validate the domain format
    2. Perform an initial scan to establish a baseline
    3. Store the project with baseline metrics

    Returns the created project with its baseline scan snapshot.

get.description:
Retrieve details for a specific project.

    Returns the project with both baseline and current scan snapshots,
    along with calculated deltas showing improvement or regression.

delete.description:
Remove a project from monitoring.

    This action is permanent and cannot be undone.
    All scan history for the project will be deleted.

get.description:
Returns all data the project detail page needs in a single call.

    Includes current/baseline scores mapped to frontend pillars,
    score history, aggregated task counts, and proof-of-value metrics.

post.description:
Create a new project from a pre-existing scan result.

    This endpoint is called by the Clerk webhook when a user signs up
    after having scanned a domain. It creates a project using the
    existing scan data as the baseline, avoiding redundant rescanning.

    Authentication: Uses internal webhook secret instead of API key.

Trigger a new scan for the project's domain.

    The scan result will:
    1. Become the new 'current_scan' snapshot
    2. Be compared against the baseline for delta calculation
    3. Increment the scan count

    Returns the new scan snapshot with changes from the previous scan.

Get AI-powered SEO recommendations for a project.

    Uses the PydanticAI SEO advisor agent to analyze scan results and provide:
    - Executive summary of current SEO state
    - Prioritized technical tasks (schema, robots.txt, llms.txt, etc.)
    - Content recommendations (blog posts, FAQs, press releases)
    - Competitive gap analysis (if competitor domain provided)
    - Top 3 priority actions
    - Estimated score improvement

    Optionally provide a competitor domain for comparative analysis.

get.description:
List all tasks for a project with optional filtering.

Tasks are returned sorted by status (todo first) then priority.
Supports both X-API-Key and X-User-Id authentication.

post.description: Create a new task for a project.

Generate SEO improvement tasks from scan recommendations.

This endpoint analyzes scan results and creates actionable tasks.
Tasks are created in 'backlog' status for review before starting.

Generate a 4-week scheduled task list from scan results.

Week structure:
- Week 1 (Foundation): Quick wins + llms.txt, robots.txt
- Week 2 (Content): Schema.org, FAQ, statistics, content structure
- Week 3 (Technical): Performance, Core Web Vitals, technical SEO
- Ongoing: Citations, agentic protocols, link building

This endpoint creates tasks organized by week with time estimates.

patch.description: Update task properties including status changes.

delete.description: Delete a task and its attempts.

post.description: Create multiple tasks at once.

patch.description:
Update multiple tasks at once.

Useful for Kanban board drag-drop operations where multiple
tasks need status or priority updates.

post.summary: Start a new attempt on a task
post.description:
Start a new attempt on a task.

Each attempt tracks work done towards completing the task.
Multiple attempts allow tracking retry history.

patch.description:
Update an attempt's status and details.

When status is 'completed', also updates the parent task to 'in_review'.

post.description:
Trigger a verification rescan for a task.

Compares before/after metrics to verify the improvement was successful.
On success, marks the task as 'verified'.

get.description: Get the latest verification result for a task.

post.summary: Execute a task via the appropriate PydanticAI agent (SSE stream)
post.description:
Execute a task via the appropriate PydanticAI agent.

Streams execution events via Server-Sent Events (SSE). The client
receives a stream of JSON-encoded events: started, step, tool_call,
message, completed, error.

get.summary: List execution runs for a task
get.description: List all execution runs for a task, most recent first.

get.summary: List log events for an execution run
get.description: List all log events for an execution run.

patch.summary: Persist verification criteria check state (Bug 2 fix)
patch.description:
Persist which verification criteria have been checked off.

The frontend toggles checkboxes on the task detail card; this endpoint
saves the set of checked labels so state survives a page reload.

Generate a query constellation for a domain.

Uses PydanticAI agent to generate ICP queries + fan-out sub-queries,
then creates MonitoredPrompt entries for each ICP query.

get.description: List all constellations for the current user.
Get a constellation with full ICP queries and fan-out data.

delete.description: Delete a constellation and deactivate its linked prompts.

post.description:
Delete the old constellation and regenerate with clean queries.

Preserves the domain, business_context, and competitor_domains from
the original, but generates entirely new ICP queries and fan-out.

Check all ICP queries in a constellation against all 5 LLM models.

Cost optimizations (Phase E):
- **Temporal caching**: Prompts checked within 7 days return cached results
  unless ``force_refresh=true``.
- **Lightweight probes**: When ``probe_mode=true`` (default), 3-5 probe
  queries run first. If the brand isn't found, the full batch is skipped
  (~80% savings for non-cited brands).
- **Engine prioritization**: Query budget is weighted by engine market share
  (ChatGPT 50%, Perplexity 25%, Gemini 15%, Claude 10%).

This triggers the existing PromptTracker.check_prompt() pipeline
for each MonitoredPrompt linked to this constellation.

parameters.name: force_refresh

schema.description: Bypass temporal cache and re-check all prompts
schema.title: Force Refresh

parameters.description: Bypass temporal cache and re-check all prompts

parameters.name: probe_mode

schema.description: Run lightweight probes before full batch
schema.title: Probe Mode

parameters.description: Run lightweight probes before full batch

get.description: Get the latest coverage snapshot for a constellation.

Per-query drill-down with all model-level check results.

Returns every ICP query in the constellation with its per-model check
results including target_mentioned, position, context, sentiment,
competitors, sources, and confidence.

Run intelligence analysis on a constellation.

Detects patterns: citation gaps, stage blind spots, competitor displacement,
structural weaknesses. Optionally generates LLM narrative (costs ~$0.01).

post.description:
Generate kanban tasks from intelligence patterns.

Runs intelligence analysis, then transforms detected patterns
into SEO kanban tasks. Returns tasks ready for insertion.

get.description:
Get longitudinal coverage trends for a constellation.

Returns historical coverage snapshots for trend visualization.

Generate a comprehensive intelligence report for a constellation.

Gathers ALL data (coverage, query results, intelligence patterns, model
diagnostics, aggregates, competitors) and produces a structured report
via LLM or rule-based fallback.

Cost: ~$0.03-0.08 per report (one LLM call with comprehensive context).

Compute citation velocity from the constellation_coverage time series.

Returns velocity_per_day, trend, momentum_score, acceleration, and
metadata about the coverage curve. Uses existing snapshot data — no LLM cost.

Detect semantically duplicate prompts using embedding cosine similarity.

Fetches existing embeddings from cache; generates missing ones via
text-embedding-3-small (if OPENAI_API_KEY is set). Falls back gracefully
with warning if no API key is available.

Classify and aggregate citation sources from prompt check results.

Returns format distribution (listicle, how-to, comparison, etc.),
competitor presence by format, and stage-level breakdowns.

Aggregate F1 citation source data into actionable content format opportunities.

Identifies stages where a dominant format (>50% of citations) exists but
the target domain is absent, suggesting high-value content creation opportunities.

post.description:
Phase 1: Scrape a domain and extract business context preview.

Returns structured data for user review/editing before Phase 2.
Cost: ~free (Jina Reader + Gemini Flash classification).

Phase 2: Generate constellation, persist prompts, create project.

Requires Phase 1 output (business_context). User may have edited the context.
Cost: ~$0.02-0.05 (one Claude Sonnet call for constellation generation).

delete.description: Remove a channel link. Called by ZeroClaw on /unlink.

post.description:
Free public ADP + Citation Readiness scan.

Rate-limited to 1 scan per IP per 24 hours (DuckDB-persisted).
Fires Loops 'audit-complete' event on success.

get.summary: Get all 44 Common Crawl metrics for a domain
get.description: Returns the full Common Crawl host index record for a domain: authority (hcrank10, prank10, raw ranks), fetch metrics (200s, 404s, redirects), nutch crawler state, robots.txt health, and content metrics (WARC record length). Covers 607M domains across 24 quarterly crawls. Source: https://data.commoncrawl.org/

Returns hcrank10, prank10, and fetch_200 across all available Common Crawl snapshots (up to 24 quarterly crawls). Use this to compute authority velocity — whether a domain is gaining or losing authority over time. Strong signal for lead gen (growing = buying signal) and retention (declining = churn risk).

get.summary: Benchmark domain against its vertical cohort
get.description: Compares a domain's authority (hcrank10) against the p50/p75/p90 of its vertical cohort. Uses the TLD as a cheap vertical proxy (e.g. '.io' domains for startups/dev tools, '.com' for general web). Returns the domain's percentile rank within the cohort.

get.summary: Calculate Crawl Health Score for a domain
get.description: Diagnoses crawl-ability problems from Common Crawl metrics. Returns a 0-100 score and a list of issues found: high error rates, crawl waste (unfetched URLs), broken robots.txt, redirect chains, thin content. Sellable as a technical SEO audit.

crawl.description: Crawl snapshot identifier, e.g. 'CC-MAIN-2024-30'. Derived from hive partition in the parquet paths.

CCMetrics.title: CCMetrics
CCMetrics.description:
All Common Crawl host index metrics for a single domain/crawl snapshot.

Columns mirror cc-host-index-schema.json exactly.  Percentage fields
are integers in the raw data (0-100 range) despite the 'pct' suffix.
Authority ranks (hcrank/prank) are floats.

found.title: Found
found.description: Whether the domain was found in CC data

quick_wins.title: Quick Wins
quick_wins.description: Easy wins for immediate score improvement

CitationReadinessV2Response.title: CitationReadinessV2Response
CitationReadinessV2Response.description:
Citation Readiness V3 (Evidence-Based) response model.

Rebalanced scoring that prioritizes content extractability and agentic
readiness over raw authority:
- Authority (20%): Domain authority
- Performance (10%): FCP < 0.4s provides citation boost
- Content (35%): Word count, structure (10) + extractability (25)
- Social (10%): Reddit mentions, review platforms (future)
- Agentic (25%): ADP + MCP + emerging protocols (future-proofing)
